items.q: What did Pollock mean by his drip paintings being 'in the painting'?
items.correct: He worked from all sides, making the act of painting central to the meaning.
items.misconception: He literally fell into wet paint.
items.why: Students take 'in the painting' literally. Pollock meant his BODILY ENGAGEMENT - working around and over the canvas on the floor - was integral to the work, not just the marks left behind.

items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.correct: An object's design should be determined by its purpose.
items.misconception: Decoration is the most important aspect of design.
items.random_wrong: Designers must follow strict government forms.
items.why: Students invert the meaning. Sullivan's modernist principle prioritises FUNCTION over ornament - the chair's shape should serve sitting, not the other way around. Postmodernism later challenged this.
The answer examiners want is: Modernist Painting. “Avant-Garde and Kitsch” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: For Greenberg, painting's essential property is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Flatness. “Narrative” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Greenberg lived from 1909 to 1994. What was his age at death (in years)?
items.why: The real figure is 85. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
